Motion To Compel: Further Responses To For Interrogatories, Further Responses To Document Requests; And Further Production Of Documents; Statement Of Questions And Answers In Dispute Re First Set Of Document Request To Plaintiffs; Statement Of Questions And Answers In Dispute Re Form Interrogatories To Plaintiffs; Declarations Of Anne E. Kears And Cathy Hankins In Support
Off Calendar Feb-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Gaetano M. Fong, Esq., for Benny Brown, Plaintiff/Opposing Party
Anne E. Kearns, for Carey Limousine of San Francisco, Inc., Defendant/Moving Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Carey Limousine of S.F. Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Further Responses To Document Requests; And Further Production Of Documents is denied as it seeks Plaintiffs' tax returns. Those returns are privileged, and there has been no waiver of that privilege.
As to Form Interrogatory 8.7, whether the motion seeks to have Plaintiff state how they calculated their categories of lost income or seeks an explanation of how those damages were calculated, the remedy is to propound further discovery because those responses adequately answer the question asked.
As to responses to Document Request Nos. 19, 23, 52, and 58, the responses must be supplemented by each Plaintiff as each must produce further documents or verify that all responsive documents have been produced. That includes any documents in the possession of accountants, except as they are a part of any tax return. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
